function.name: run_shell
function.description: Run a bash command. Returns stdout/stderr (separated in meta as 'stdout'/'stderr' fields). Truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB. Prefixed with [exit: N], classification, and optional [hint: ...] for recovery. Error responses include error_code.

force.description: If true, execute commands even when risk classification is 'dangerous'. Default false.

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read file contents with line numbers. Up to 2000 lines per call. Use start_line/end_line for large files. All error responses include error_code and suggestion fields.

tail.description: Read the last N lines of the file. Takes precedence over start_line/end_line. 0 = disabled.

line_numbers.description: Include cat-n style line-number prefixes in output (default: true). Set false to receive raw file content with no numbering.

truncate.description: Strategy when output exceeds line limit. head=keep first N (default, paginates with start_line). tail=keep last N (logs). middle=keep both ends, elide middle. none=defer to byte cap only. smart=brace-depth heuristic for C-syntax files, falls back to head for others.

include_checksum.description: When true, response meta includes sha256 hex digest of the full file content. Zero extra I/O cost (computed during read).

if_checksum.description: SHA-256 hex digest from a previous read_file response (meta.sha256). When set and the file's current checksum matches, returns {"unchanged":true,"path":"...","checksum":"..."} instead of file content — saves tokens in build→fix→build loops. Mismatched or absent checksum falls through to a normal full read.

function.name: multi_read
function.description: Read multiple files in one call. Returns a JSON object with {files: {path: content}, errors: {path: {error, suggestion, error_code}}, truncation: {path: {truncated, total_lines, output_lines}}}. Supports start_line/end_line/tail per file. Failed individual reads are reported in the errors map without failing the entire call.

files.description: List of file read requests. Each entry is an object with path and optional windowing.

tail.description: Read last N lines. Overrides start_line/end_line. 0=disabled.

truncate.description: Truncation strategy. smart=brace-depth heuristic for C-syntax files.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file (creates parent dirs). Atomic via temp+rename.

function.name: edit_file
function.description: Replace exact text in a file. old_text must appear exactly once. On failure, error_code indicates the cause (edit_not_found, edit_not_unique, edit_no_change, etc.). Response meta includes matchType ('exact'|'fuzzy'), fuzzyNormalized, firstChangedLine, and lastChangedLine.

old_text.description: exact text to find (must be unique in file)

fuzzy_indent.description: auto-detect indentation at match site and apply to new_text (default: false)

show_context.description: Number of context lines to show around the edit after applying. 0 = no context.

function.name: multi_edit
function.description: Apply multiple edits across files. All edits are validated first (collect-then-report); if validation fails, none are applied. Writes are per-file atomic and are not rolled back if a later write fails. Failures include per-edit error details with error_code per edit. Response meta includes matchType and line range.

old_text.description: exact text to find (must be unique)

fuzzy_indent.description: Re-indent old_text to match surrounding indentation before matching

show_context.description: Number of context lines to show around the edit after applying

dry_run.description: Preview all edits without writing. Returns diffs for each edit.

function.name: apply_patch
function.description: Apply a Codex-style patch (*** Begin Patch ... *** End Patch) to create, delete, or update files. Supports add-file, delete-file, and update-file operations with hunk-based editing using @@ context markers and space/minus/plus prefixed lines. All operations are validated before writing; writes are per-file atomic and are not rolled back if a later write fails.

patch.description: Codex-style patch payload. Must start with '*** Begin Patch' and end with '*** End Patch'. Supports *** Add File:, *** Delete File:, and *** Update File: operations.

function.name: search_files
function.description: Search file contents with grep. Returns file:line:match. Default limit is 500 matches. When zero results, response includes zero_match_reason ('invalid_regex', 'path_not_found', 'path_is_empty_dir', or 'pattern_matched_nothing'). Error responses include error_code.

pattern.description: grep regex pattern (or literal string when literal=true)

literal.description: treat pattern as a fixed string rather than a regex (default: false)

max_matches.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 500). When exceeded, response includes truncated=true and total_count.

format.description: output format: 'text' (default), 'json' (structured results with truncation metadata), or 'filenames' (filenames with match counts)

function.name: stat_file
function.description: Get file metadata without reading contents. Returns size, line count, modification time, type, encoding (utf-8|binary), line_ending (lf|crlf|mixed), and bom (none|utf-8-bom|utf-16-le|utf-16-be) for regular files.

function.name: list_dir
function.description: List directory contents (pure Go, no subprocess). Hidden files excluded. Default limit is 500 entries (max 10000). Supports mtime filtering.

max_entries.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 500, cap: 10000). When exceeded, response includes truncated=true and total_count.

changed_since.description: Unix epoch seconds. Only list entries modified after this timestamp. 0 or absent = no filter.

function.name: find_files
function.description: Find files by glob pattern. Uses fd when available (respects .gitignore), falls back to POSIX find. Returns matching file paths relative to workdir. Default limit is 1000 results.

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files, e.g. '*.go', '**/*.json', or 'src/**/*_test.go'

path.description: Directory to search in (default: current directory)

function.name: list_tools
function.description: List tool capability metadata. Pass include_schema=true only for runtime schema discovery.

include_schema.description: Also include the compact OpenAI tool schema (default: false).

function.name: checksum_tree
function.description: Compute SHA-256 hashes for a file tree. Returns JSON map of {path: hash}. Supports differential mode with baseline comparison.

baseline.description: Optional map of {path: sha256} from a previous run. When provided, response includes changed/added/removed arrays and only returns hashes for changed files.

function.name: detect_project
function.description: Detect project language, framework, build tool, test command, and linter from config files.

function.name: memory
function.description: Persistent key/value memory for the agent, scoped per project. Save, recall, list, or forget keys across sessions. Stored in a SQLite DB at ~/.config/jinn/memory.db (or $JINN_CONFIG_DIR/jinn/memory.db). Keys are isolated per project (auto-detected from the nearest .git ancestor); use scope="global" for a cross-project bucket.

key.description: Key name (1-128 chars, [a-zA-Z0-9_.-]). Required for save, recall, forget.

value.description: Value to store (max 16 KiB). Required for save.

scope.description: Optional. Omit for the current project (auto-detected). Use "global" for the cross-project bucket, or an absolute path for an explicit project. Junk (relative) values are rejected.

include_values.description: list only. When true, returns {entries:[{key,value,updated}],count:N} instead of {keys:[...],count:N}. Default false.

function.name: undo
function.description: Manage file mutation history. Snapshots are captured automatically before write_file, edit_file, and multi_edit operations. Use list to browse, preview to diff, restore to revert.

action.description: Operation: list=show history, preview=diff snapshot vs current, restore=revert file, clear=delete all history for this workdir.

id.description: Snapshot ID (prefix match). Required for preview and restore.

limit.description: Max entries to return for list (default: 50).

function.name: lsp_query
function.description: Query a language server for semantic info. Supports: gopls (.go), rust-analyzer (.rs), pylsp (.py), typescript-language-server (.ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx), clangd (.c/.h/.cpp/.cc/.cxx/.hpp), jdtls (.java), lua-language-server (.lua), zls (.zig). Auto-detects from file extension.

action.description: Query type. diagnostics returns pull diagnostics; rename returns a preview of changes (does not apply them).

path.description: Path to source file, relative to workDir.

character.description: 1-based character offset within the line. Required for definition/references/hover/rename unless symbol is provided.

symbol.description: Symbol name to find on the line. When provided and character is absent, auto-detects the column by searching for this string on the given line.

new_name.description: New name for the symbol. Required when action is rename.

function.name: diff_files
function.description: Compare two files and return a unified diff. Uses the same diff engine as edit_file preview. Response meta includes is_identical (bool) and first_changed_line (int).

function.name: search_replace
function.description: Regex-based search and replace across files. Supports capture groups ($1, $2) in replacement, multi-file scope (glob patterns), replace-all (every occurrence), validate-first apply, and per-file atomic writes. Use when edit_file/multi_edit cannot: regex patterns, multi-file bulk changes, or replacing all occurrences. Binary files are skipped automatically.

pattern.description: Regex pattern to search for. Supports capture groups referenced as $1, $2, etc. in replacement. Default flags: multiline (m) — ^/$ match line boundaries.

replacement.description: Replacement text. Supports $1, $2, etc. for capture group references. Use empty string for deletion.

files.description: Target files: a single path, glob pattern, or array of paths/globs (max 50 files).

include.description: Optional glob filter applied to resolved file paths (e.g. '*.go'). Only files matching this filter are processed.

multiline.description: Multiline mode: ^/$ match line boundaries (default: true)

dry_run.description: Preview all replacements without writing. Returns diffs and match counts per file (default: false)

function.name: task
function.description: Manage continuity tasks (create, begin, update status, get, list). Tasks are persisted in the local jinn DB and survive across sessions.

task_id.description: Task ID. Required for begin, set_status, get.

description.description: Optional task description. Used with create.

priority.description: Priority (default 0, higher = more important). Used with create.

status.description: Target status. Required for set_status. Used with list as a filter.

blocked_reason.description: Free-form reason why the task is blocked. Used with set_status when status=blocked.

project_id.description: Project ID (repo path). Defaults to auto-detected repo root. Used with create and list.

agent.description: Agent identity. Defaults to JINN_CLIENT env var or 'agent'.

function.name: event
function.description: Append to or list the continuity event log. Events are the append-only activity stream for tasks and projects.

kind.description: Event kind (max 128 chars). Required for append. E.g. 'progress', 'error', 'tool_call'.

message.description: Event message (max 4096 chars). Required for append.

task_id.description: Associate event with this task ID. Optional for append; filter for list.

project_id.description: Project ID. Defaults to auto-detected repo root for append; filter for list.

metadata.description: Optional JSON object or JSON string (max 16384 bytes). Used with append.

agent.description: Agent identity for append. Defaults to JINN_CLIENT env var or 'agent'.

limit.description: Max events to return (default 20, cap 100). Used with list.

since_id.description: Return only events with id > since_id (pagination). Used with list.

function.name: resume
function.description: Resume work: select a focus task via deterministic 5-rule selection and return a brief packet (task, project, relevant memory, recent events, artifacts, task counts, pipeline, deltas). Advances the agent cursor and persists focus atomically unless peek=true.

agent.description: Agent identity. Defaults to JINN_CLIENT env var or 'agent'.

project_id.description: Project ID (repo path) to scope focus selection and counts. Defaults to auto-detected repo root.

peek.description: If true, build the brief WITHOUT advancing the cursor or persisting focus (idempotent read). Default false.
